SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF FEB-06-2020 - CONTINUED FOR WITNESS AND ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE TO JUN-01-2020 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 506. PLAINTIFF EDDIE STEVENSON IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT/TARAVAL STATION REPRESENTED BY NICHELLE FLENTROY FOR CCSF IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT SARGENT RICHARD D. SANTIAGO IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT/TARAVEL STATION, REPRESENTED BY NICHELLE FLENTROY OF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE OFFICER COLLINS AND OFFICER GODFREY AT THIS HEARING. FURTHERMORE, CCSF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE THE FOLLOWING AT THE HEARING: THE PERSONNEL RECORDS OF OFFICER COLLINS, WHICH ARE TO BE REVIEWED "IN CAMERA" BY JUDGE ROBERTSON. THESE RECORDS ARE TO INCLUDE COMPLAINTS ABOUT OFFICER COLLINS WRITING PARKING TICKETS. SARGENT SANTIGO IS TO PROVIDE THE RAP SHEET HE REVIEWED AS TO PLAINTIFF EDDIE STEVENSON. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT EXHIBITS RETAINED. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: W. TRUPEK. NOT REPORTED.(506)
ORDER CASE CONTINUED FOR ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE. (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT FOR DETAILS.)

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL CALENDAR OF MAY-12-2021 - SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT REVERSED. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPT. DOES NOT OWE EDDIE STEVENSON ANY MONEY. THE JUDGMENT ON APPEAL IS FINAL AND REPLACES THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT OF JANUARY 19, 2021. EXHIBITS WILL BE DESTROYED PURSUANT TO CCP 1952.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT APPELLANT SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT/TARAVEL STATION TAKE NOTHING FROM RESPONDENT STEVENSON, EDDIE SEE GOVT. CODE SEC. 821.6; JOHNSON V.CITY OF PACIFICA (1970) 4 CA3D 82; RANDLE V. CCSF (1986) 186 CA3D 449; COLLINS V. CCSF (1975) 50 CA3D 671. RESPONDENT BROUGHT NO FEDERAL CLAIM IN SMALL CLAIMS COURT. SEE JUDGMENT ATTACHED NO EXHIBITS FILED NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND CERTIFICATE OF MAILING FILED

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Judgment Entered

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Apr-14-2021 Continued To May-12-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eddie Stevenson, Plaintiff/Respondent; Brian Stevens for the City and County of San Francisco [San Francisco Police Dept.], Defendant/Appellant.

The Court notes receipt of briefing from Defendant/Respondent concerning the state of the law with respect to the liability, or immunity therefrom, of public employees for actions within the scope of their employment. The Court would give Plaintiff Stevenson 20 court days [May 12, 2021] to file a responsive brief to the Appellant's contention that the City and its employees are immune from liability when acting within the scope of their employmen citing Gov't Code Sec. 821.6. The Court will determine at that time whether further evidenciary hearing is required.

Court adjourns.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D306)
